message:
chore(beads): refresh stdio/rtld/epic tracker and close host-parity abi tests

Sync the beads/issues.jsonl store to reflect today's scope & status
changes that were made through br:

Opened (epics / planning):
- bd-10pq: EPIC — cross-cutting architecture heterogeneity coverage
  (aarch64, x86_64, RISC-V parity tracker) so arch-specific blockers
  are visible to br ready.
- bd-13zp: EPIC — killer demo for the memory-safety membrane, umbrella
  for the showcase deliverable.
- bd-18qq: EPIC — testing and fault-injection campaign, umbrella for
  the ongoing fuzz / differential / adversarial work.
- bd-1x3: EPIC — stub extermination ranked by observed live call
  traces (drives picking the highest-impact unimplemented syscalls).
- bd-3rn: rtld phase-1 — ELF relocation + symbol lookup core for the
  non-IFUNC path (stepping stone to dynamic linking parity).

Opened (scoped stdio subtasks under bd-9chy):
- bd-9chy.4: versioned JSONL evidence schema + parser + ring buffer.
- bd-9chy.7: native tmpfile/tmpfile64 via O_TMPFILE + native fdopen.
- bd-9chy.8: complete native fmemopen / open_memstream /
  open_wmemstream with NativeFile memory backing.
- bd-9chy.10: export _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_ aliases bound to
  NativeFile storage so glibc-compatible programs link cleanly.
- bd-9chy.26: offline synthesis pipeline (tools/stdio_synth/) for
  printf/scanf grammar + table compile.

Status changes:
- bd-l2yj: moved to in_progress — investigation of the
  pthread_sigqueue_preserves_siginfo_and_ucontext_after_deferred_replay
  ESRCH regression is now active.

Closed (host-parity abi test coverage already landed):
- bd-t3rb: host-parity errno contracts for setns and unshare.
- bd-y0wo: host-parity errno contracts for pthread_condattr_setclock.

Pure issue-tracker bookkeeping — no source code changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
